tipRules.tip: Robertson: Use a 7-inch swimbait on a 1-ounce jighead to target big bass on offshore hard-bottom spots with no visible structure, casting to fish spotted on electronics in 9-10 feet of water.
Robertson: Pair a SPRO Little John XL crankbait with big swimbaits and spinnerbaits when targeting offshore bass on featureless hard-bottom areas in 9-10 feet of water.
Robertson: Include a big spinnerbait in your offshore rotation alongside swimbaits and crankbaits when targeting bass grouped on hard-bottom spots.

knowledge.insight: Robertson: On fisheries known for shallow grass fishing like the Harris Chain, look for offshore hard-bottom spots 150 yards from shore in 9-10 feet of water. Idle over the area and use electronics to spot big bass — a 40x50 yard hard spot can hold fish that replenish daily.

Robertson: If you're an offshore fisherman, commit to your style even on fisheries known for shallow patterns. When a shallow approach fails, go back to what you do well — if you're going to die, die doing what you do well.
Robertson: Idle every inch of a lake during practice to find offshore spots. Look for hard-bottom areas where you can see big bass on electronics, even if you can't get them to bite in practice.
